narrative: Your loan application for 1108 Deutsche Marks to cover repairs over a 12-month period has been carefully reviewed by our credit model, which predicted a 51.5% probability that this could turn into a bad credit risk—meaning it's slightly more likely than not that repayment might face challenges, leading to the denial of your request. As a 25-year-old male single foreign worker with a skilled job, 4-7 years of employment, your own housing, real estate property, and a small dependent, you've built some stability, including no other installment plans or debtors involved. However, the model focused on a few key aspects of your financial profile that raised concerns, with your checking account status showing less than 0 DM standing out as the strongest factor pushing toward a higher risk prediction, as empty or overdrawn accounts often signal short-term cash flow issues that worry lenders. Close behind was your credit history of no prior credits or all paid ones, which the model saw as limited evidence of proven repayment reliability, unlike applicants with a track record of handling loans successfully. On a more positive note, your real estate property worked in your favor, pulling the prediction toward good credit by demonstrating valuable assets that could back repayment—yet it wasn't enough to outweigh the top concerns. Compared to typical approved applicants, who often have healthier checking balances and more established credit histories, your situation appeared riskier in those areas despite strengths like your low 4% installment rate on disposable income and just two existing credits at our bank. This combination tipped the scales, even with positives like owning your home and steady work. To improve your chances next time, consider building up your checking account balance, perhaps by saving a bit more—your <100 DM savings status might help here—and gaining experience with smaller credits to strengthen your history. You could also explore adding a guarantor or documenting any reliable income patterns from your skilled role. Reapplying after addressing these, maybe with a shorter duration or smaller amount, often leads to better outcomes for profiles like yours. We're here to support you in taking these steps toward approval.
